chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-3143 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 2. For Katcha Peshi. 3. For hearing of MA 13144/17 23.01.2018. Mr. Ghulam Mustafa Abbasi, Advocate for petitioner. Respondent No.2 present in person. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, A.A.G. After hearing the learned Counsel for the parties at some length learned Counsel for the petitioner submits that he would be satisfied and shall not press this Constitutional Petition if petitioner is allowed to file application for premature acquittal before the trial court for passing appropriate order in accordance with law. Learned A.A.G has raised his no objection to the above proposition. In view of the above, this Constitutional Petition stands disposed of alongwith listed application. However, petitioner would be at liberty to file application under section 265-K, Cr.P.C if he so desires for redressal of his grievance and in case said application is filed, the learned trial court is directed to decide the same in accordance with law after hearing the parties without influence of the order passed by learned Judicial Magistrate dated 08.09.2017. JUDGE JUDGE Ali Haider
